question_text: Which of the following best explains why aircraft are fitted with conducting strips called 'static dischargers' on their wings and tail?

options.label: A
options.text: They keep the aircraft cool during flight

options.label: B
options.text: They allow static charge built up from air friction to leak away gradually into the air, preventing dangerous spark discharge

options.label: C
options.text: They attract lightning away from the engines

options.label: D
options.text: They reduce air resistance and improve fuel efficiency

explanation: Aircraft moving through the air experience friction that builds up static charge. Static dischargers are thin conducting wires that bleed this charge away slowly into the surrounding air, preventing the dangerous buildup that could cause a spark near flammable fuel vapours.
